hints.potentes: 'Potentes' is a substantive adjective meaning 'the mighty'

steps.reveal: deposuit = he has put down / he deposed

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'deponit'; 'deposuit' uses the perfect stem

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the principal parts of 'pono' (to place); 'depono' is a compound
hints.dispono, disponere, disposui, dispositum: These are the principal parts of 'dispono' (to arrange)
hints.compono, componere, composui, compositum: These are the principal parts of 'compono' (to compose)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural is also 'potentes', but here it is the direct object of 'deposuit'

hints.1st/2nd declension adjective: 1st/2nd declension adjectives end in -us/-a/-um; 'potens, potentis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th declension: Adjectives do not follow the 4th declension pattern
hints.indeclinable: 'Potens' clearly declines: potens, potentis, potenti, potentem, potenti

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'sedes'; 'sede' with -e is ablative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'sedes' has genitive 'sedis'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us; 'sedes' has genitive 'sedis'
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive -ei; 'sedes' has genitive 'sedis'

translation: He has put down the mighty from their thrones
